title: Next.js App Router Patterns for Marketing Sites
excerpt: How we structure content pages, CMS fallbacks, and static generation for fast marketing sites.
author: Anh Th

sections.heading: Route groups and layouts
sections.content: We isolate marketing pages under a site route group with shared navbar and footer layouts, keeping studio and API routes separate from public content.

sections.heading: CMS with static fallback
sections.content: Every content type ships with typed static data and a Sanity resolver. Builds succeed without CMS credentials while editors can override content in production.

sections.heading: Static generation strategy
sections.content: Detail pages use generateStaticParams from static slugs so deploys stay fast and predictable, with on-demand revalidation added when needed.
